L1.area: Expression evaluation engine and its runtime safety enforcement layer

L2.description: A time-of-check time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerability exists in the expression evaluation sandbox where mutable AST node properties are checked for safety and then used in a subsequent operation. An attacker who can control input to the expression evaluator can manipulate the checked property between the safety verification and the actual use, bypassing attribute access restrictions and achieving arbitrary code execution within the host application's context.

L3.area: Attribute access evaluation and string formatting within the expression interpreter's AST node handlers
L3.description: A time-of-check time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerability exists in the expression evaluation sandbox's attribute access handler where mutable AST node attribute names are checked against a safety blocklist and then used in a subsequent attribute retrieval call. An attacker can manipulate the AST node's attribute name between the safety check and the retrieval, changing it to a restricted dunder attribute. Additionally, string formatting operations within the evaluator do not enforce the same attribute access restrictions, providing another vector for sandbox escape.

ground_truth.reason: The on_attribute handler checks node.attr against a safety blocklist but then uses the same mutable property in a getattr call, allowing an attacker to swap the attribute name between check and use to access restricted dunder methods and escape the sandbox.
